# Skills Catalog (auto-generated)

_Generated by `tools/scripts/skills_index.sh` — do not edit by hand._

One line per skill. Read `skills/{name}/SKILL.md` for full workflow.

- **cis-controls** — Use when a user wants to align a configuration, design, integration, implementation plan, or operational workflow to the CIS Controls v8.1, or asks which CIS controls apply to a proposed change. _(see `skills/cis-controls/SKILL.md`)_
- **code-review** — Use when reviewing changes for bugs, regressions, edge cases, missing tests, risky assumptions, or maintainability issues. Prioritize findings over summary. _(see `skills/code-review/SKILL.md`)_
- **feature-spec-writer** — Use when a rough product idea needs to become an implementation-ready spec with user goals, constraints, scope, edge cases, acceptance criteria, and delivery notes. _(see `skills/feature-spec-writer/SKILL.md`)_
- **incident-response** — Use during or after a security or operational incident to drive structured triage, containment, eradication, recovery, and lessons learned. Produces a written incident record using templates/incident-response-template.md. Lessons feed back into memory/effectus/wisdom.md and memory/security/incident-log.md. _(see `skills/incident-response/SKILL.md`)_
- **planning** — Use when a task needs decomposition into phases, checkpoints, risks, or implementation order. Best for turning broad goals into a concrete execution plan without over-specifying every step. _(see `skills/planning/SKILL.md`)_
- **repo-onboarding** — Use when entering an unfamiliar codebase and you need a quick, reliable understanding of structure, entry points, commands, risks, and where to make changes. _(see `skills/repo-onboarding/SKILL.md`)_
- **research-synthesizer** — Use when gathering information from multiple sources and converting it into a concise, decision-ready synthesis with source-aware confidence and clear next steps. _(see `skills/research-synthesizer/SKILL.md`)_
- **security-review** — Use when reviewing code, configuration, or infrastructure changes for security risk. Covers authn, authz, input validation, secrets handling, dependency risk, error handling, logging, and crypto. Prioritize findings over summary; cite file:line on every finding. _(see `skills/security-review/SKILL.md`)_
- **threat-modeling** — Use when designing a new system, feature, or integration that handles sensitive data, crosses trust boundaries, or exposes an attack surface. Produces a structured threat model with STRIDE enumeration, attack trees for high-impact threats, and proposed mitigations. Output goes to templates/threat-model-template.md format. _(see `skills/threat-modeling/SKILL.md`)_
